dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4534 IS: Microbusiness Support Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Ms. Cortez Masto (for herself and Ms. Alsobrooks) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship

form.official-title: To amend the Small Business Act to establish a direct loan program for microbusinesses at the Small Business Administration, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Microbusiness Support Act.
Section 7(a) of the Small Business Act (15 U.S.C. 636(a)) is amended by adding at the end the following:

clause.text: In this paragraph, the term microbusiness means an independently owned and operated for-profit business entity that—

subclause.text: employs not more than 10 full-time employees, determined on a full-time equivalent basis; and
has annual revenue of not more than the lesser of—

item.text: the size standard in dollars, if any, for the North American Industry Classification System code assigned to the business entity for the business entity to qualify as a small business concern.

clause.text: For purposes of clause (i), the term full-time means that an individual—

subclause.text: is employed for consideration for not less than 35 hours each week; or
renders any other standard of service generally accepted by custom or specified by contract as full-time employment.

clause.text: The Administrator may request from a business entity such documentation as may be necessary to establish that the business entity qualifies as a microbusiness under this subparagraph.

subparagraph.text: The Administrator is authorized to originate and disburse direct loans, including through partnerships with third parties, to microbusinesses under this subsection.
The maximum amount of a loan made under this paragraph to a microbusiness is $100,000.
With respect to each loan made under this paragraph, the Administrator, an authorized third party, or an agent may—

clause.text: impose, collect, retain, and utilize fees, which may be charged to the borrower, to cover any costs associated with referring applications or originating, making, underwriting, disbursing, closing, servicing, or liquidating the loan, including any direct lending agent costs, other program or contract costs, or other agent administrative expenses;
impose, collect, retain, and utilize fees (including unused fees and draw fees), which may be charged to the borrower on loans for revolving lines of credit; and

pay third parties, including direct lending agents and financial institutions, with which the Administration partners for assistance in referring applicants or promoting, originating, making, underwriting, disbursing, closing, servicing, or liquidating loans in accordance with this paragraph on behalf of the Administration.

Not later than 90 days after the date of enactment of this paragraph, the Administrator shall issue interim final rules and revise any relevant rules to establish the terms and conditions for a direct loan made under this paragraph, including with respect to repayment, underwriting criteria, interest rate, maturity, and other terms.
The interest rate for a loan made under this paragraph shall be in accordance with paragraph (4)(A), except 6 percent per annum shall be substituted for 1 percent per annum.
